X:ADMIT.notes: Allows a previously-blocked state through the admissibility predicate.

X:REJECT.notes: Denies a state; raises X boundary pressure but lowers manifold contradiction.

X:RECLASSIFY.notes: Moves a state to a different admissibility class without destroying it.

X:RESOLVE_CONTRADICTION.notes: Collapses two contradicting states into one admissible state; high X-risk.

T:DEFER.notes: Pushes an operation to a later tick; relieves immediate T pressure.

T:BATCH.notes: Groups multiple deferred operations into one sequence pass.

T:REORDER.notes: Changes the execution order of pending operations.

T:SIM_TICK.notes: Advance internal simulation clock by one unit; minimal cost.

N:REUSE.notes: Re-uses existing computed state; lowers N expenditure.

N:CACHE.notes: Stores result for future reuse; upfront B and T cost.

N:REDUCE_STATE.notes: Removes redundant state nodes; lowers ongoing N overhead.

N:SPEND.notes: Deliberate energy expenditure for high-value relief elsewhere.

B:SEPARATE.notes: Draws a clean boundary between two previously coupled regions.

B:ENCAPSULATE.notes: Turns raw state into bounded envelope; lowers boundary leakage if successful.

B:ROUTE.notes: Directs pressure flow through an established channel.

B:SEAL.notes: Freezes a boundary to prevent further leakage.

A:COMMIT.notes: Locks in a chosen path; high A cost paid upfront.

A:CHOOSE.notes: Evaluates branch options and selects one.

A:ASSERT.notes: Stakes a claim on current state as valid; raises X exposure briefly.

A:OUTLET_PUSH.notes: Expels pressure through external outlet; relief only if outlet confirms effectiveness.
